nouns.home.bed: A bed is where you sleep at night.
It is soft, with a pillow and a cozy blanket.
You climb into bed when it is time to rest.
nouns.home.bath: A bath is where you wash your whole body.
It is a big tub you fill with warm, bubbly water.
You take a bath to get clean and splash.
nouns.home.cup: A cup is what you drink from.
It is small and round and holds water or juice.
You hold a cup with your hands and sip.
nouns.home.spoon: You use a spoon to scoop and eat soft food.
It has a small round bowl on the end of a handle.
You eat soup, yogurt, or cereal with a spoon.
nouns.home.fork: You use a fork to pick up food and bring it to your mouth.
It has long pointy parts at the end called tines.
You eat with a fork at the table, next to a spoon.
nouns.home.plate: A plate is what you put your food on.
It is flat and round and holds your meal.
Your food sits on a plate at the table.
nouns.home.table: A table is where you sit to eat or play.
It is flat on top with legs holding it up.
You put your plate on the table and sit at it.
nouns.home.chair: A chair is what you sit on.
It has a seat, a back, and four legs.
You pull up a chair to sit at the table.
nouns.home.door: A door is what you open to go in and out.
It is flat and tall with a handle to turn.
You open the door to go into a room.
nouns.home.light: A light helps you see when it is dark.
It glows bright when you flip the switch.
You turn on the light when a room is dark.
nouns.home.tv: A TV is a screen where you watch shows.
It is a big flat screen with pictures and sound.
You watch your favorite shows on the TV.
nouns.home.blanket: A blanket keeps you warm and cozy.
It is soft, and you snuggle under it.
You pull up a blanket when you sleep or rest.
nouns.home.pillow: A pillow is soft, and you rest your head on it.
It is a squishy cushion for your head.
You put your head on a pillow when you sleep.
nouns.home.towel: A towel dries you off after a bath.
It is soft and fluffy and soaks up water.
You wrap up in a towel when you are wet.
nouns.home.soap: Soap helps you wash and get clean.
It makes bubbles and can be a bar or a squirt.
You scrub with soap when you wash your hands.
nouns.home.kitchen.refrigerator: A refrigerator keeps food cold and fresh.
It is a big cold box with shelves and doors.
You open the fridge to get milk or a snack.
nouns.home.kitchen.oven: An oven bakes and cooks food with heat.
It is hot inside — careful, do not touch.
Cookies and dinner get baked in the oven.
nouns.home.kitchen.stove: A stove cooks food in pots and pans.
Its top gets hot — careful, do not touch.
Soup and eggs get cooked on the stove.
nouns.home.kitchen.microwave: A microwave warms up food fast.
It is a little box with a door and buttons.
You heat up leftovers in the microwave.
nouns.home.kitchen.sink: A sink is where you wash with water.
It has a faucet that pours water into a basin.
You wash hands and dishes at the sink.
nouns.home.kitchen.dishwasher: A dishwasher washes the dirty dishes for you.
You load plates and cups inside and close it.
The dishwasher cleans the dishes with water.
nouns.home.kitchen.blender: A blender mixes food into a smooth drink.
It has a tall cup and sharp spinning blades — careful.
You make smoothies in a blender.
nouns.home.kitchen.toaster: A toaster makes bread warm and crispy.
You drop bread in the slots, and toast pops up.
You make toast in the toaster for breakfast.
nouns.home.kitchen.bowl: A bowl holds food like cereal or soup.
It is round and deep so food does not spill.
You eat cereal from a bowl with a spoon.
nouns.home.kitchen.pot: A pot is used to cook food on the stove.
It is deep with a handle and sometimes a lid.
You cook soup or pasta in a pot.
nouns.home.kitchen.pan: A pan is used to cook food on the stove.
It is flat with low sides and a handle.
You cook eggs or pancakes in a pan.
nouns.home.kitchen.knife: A knife is used to cut food — it is sharp, so careful.
It has a sharp edge and a handle to hold.
A grown-up uses a knife to cut your food.
nouns.home.mealtime.napkin: A napkin wipes your hands and mouth.
It is a soft square of paper or cloth.
You use a napkin to clean up while you eat.
nouns.home.mealtime.straw: A straw helps you sip your drink.
It is a thin tube you put in your cup.
You suck through a straw to drink.
nouns.home.mealtime.bib: A bib keeps your shirt clean while you eat.
It ties around your neck and covers your chest.
You wear a bib so food does not spill on you.
nouns.home.mealtime.sippy_cup: A sippy cup is a kid's cup that does not spill.
It has a lid with a little spout to drink from.
You drink from a sippy cup without making a mess.
nouns.home.mealtime.water_bottle: A water bottle holds water to drink anywhere.
It has a lid that keeps the water inside.
You bring a water bottle to school or the park.
nouns.home.mealtime.mug: A mug is a cup with a handle.
It is round with a handle to hold.
You drink warm milk or cocoa from a mug.
nouns.home.mealtime.tray: A tray carries food and dishes.
It is flat with little edges to hold things.
You carry your snack on a tray.
nouns.home.mealtime.pitcher: A pitcher holds a drink and pours it out.
It is tall with a handle and a spout.
You pour juice from a pitcher into a cup.
nouns.home.mealtime.placemat: A placemat goes under your plate.
It is a flat mat that keeps the table clean.
Your plate and cup sit on a placemat.
nouns.home.mealtime.lunchbox: It is a box with a handle that holds your food.
You take your lunchbox to school.
nouns.home.mealtime.thermos: A thermos keeps a drink warm or cold.
It is a tall cup with a tight lid.
You bring soup or a drink in a thermos.
nouns.home.bathroom.toothbrush: It has a handle and tiny bristles on the end.
You brush with it in the morning and at night.
nouns.home.bathroom.toothpaste: Toothpaste goes on your toothbrush to clean teeth.
It is a minty paste you squeeze from a tube.
You put toothpaste on your brush before you brush.
nouns.home.bathroom.comb: It is flat with lots of little teeth.
You run a comb through your hair to fix it.
nouns.home.bathroom.brush: It has a handle and lots of bristles.
You brush your hair with it to make it neat.
nouns.home.bathroom.mirror: It is shiny, and you see yourself in it.
You look in the mirror to see your face.
nouns.home.bathroom.shampoo: It is a soapy liquid that makes bubbles.
You use shampoo in the bath to wash your hair.
nouns.home.bathroom.shower: A shower sprays water to wash you.
Warm water rains down from above.
You stand under the shower to get clean.
nouns.home.bathroom.faucet: A faucet is where the water comes out.
You turn the handle and water flows.
You turn on the faucet to wash your hands.
nouns.home.tech.phone: A phone is used to call and talk to people.
It is a small screen you hold in your hand.
You can call Grandma on the phone.
nouns.home.tech.laptop: A laptop is a computer that folds shut.
It has a screen and a keyboard with letters.
Grown-ups type and work on a laptop.
nouns.home.tech.tablet: A tablet is a flat screen you tap and swipe.
It is a touchscreen with games and videos.
You can play and learn on a tablet.
nouns.home.tech.headphones: Headphones let you listen without bothering others.
They go over your ears and play sound just for you.
You wear headphones to hear music or a show.
nouns.home.tech.charger: A charger fills up the battery.
It is a cord you plug into the wall.
You plug in the charger when the battery is low.
nouns.home.tech.remote: A remote controls the TV from your seat.
It has lots of little buttons you press.
You press the remote to change the show.
nouns.home.tech.camera: It has a lens and a button you press to snap.
You use a camera to take a photo.
nouns.home.tech.button: A button is a little spot you press to make something happen.
It is small and round, and it clicks.
You press a button to turn things on.
nouns.home.tech.screen: A screen is the flat part that shows pictures.
It lights up with videos, games, and words.
You look at the screen to watch and play.
nouns.home.tech.speaker: A speaker plays sound out loud.
It is a box that music and voices come from.
You hear music from a speaker.
nouns.body.hand: Your hand is at the end of your arm.
It has five fingers for holding, waving, and clapping.
You use your hands to pick things up and play.
nouns.body.foot: Your foot is at the bottom of your leg.
It has five toes and helps you stand and walk.
You put a shoe on each foot.
nouns.body.head: Your head is on top of your body.
It holds your face, your hair, and your brain.
You nod your head for yes and shake it for no.
nouns.body.tummy: Your tummy is the soft part in the middle of you.
It is where your food goes when you eat.
Your tummy rumbles when you are hungry.
nouns.body.eye: You have two eyes, and they blink.
You use your eyes to look at the world.
nouns.body.ear: You have one on each side of your head.
You listen to sounds with your ears.
nouns.body.nose: Your nose is in the middle of your face.
You smell with it and breathe through it.
Your nose tells you if something smells yummy.
nouns.body.mouth: Your mouth is how you eat and talk.
It has lips, teeth, and a tongue inside.
You use your mouth to chew, smile, and speak.
nouns.body.hair: Your hair grows on top of your head.
It can be short or long, curly or straight.
You brush and wash your hair to keep it neat.
nouns.body.arm: Your arm reaches out from your shoulder to your hand.
It bends at the elbow and helps you reach and hug.
You have two arms for carrying and lifting.
nouns.body.leg: Your leg goes from your hip down to your foot.
It bends at the knee and helps you walk and run.
You have two legs for standing and jumping.
nouns.body.teeth: Your teeth are the hard white parts in your mouth.
You use them to bite and chew your food.
You brush your teeth to keep them clean.
nouns.body.more.finger: A finger is one of the parts on your hand.
You have five fingers, and they can wiggle.
You point and pick up small things with your fingers.
nouns.body.more.toe: A toe is one of the parts on your foot.
You have five toes on each foot, and they wiggle.
Your toes help you balance when you stand.
nouns.body.more.knee: Your knee is in the middle of your leg.
It bends so you can sit, crawl, and jump.
You might get an owie on your knee if you fall.
nouns.body.more.elbow: Your elbow is in the middle of your arm.
It bends so you can reach your mouth and wave.
Your arm folds at the elbow.
nouns.body.more.shoulder: Your shoulder is where your arm meets your body.
It is at the top of your arm, near your neck.
You can shrug your shoulders up and down.
nouns.body.more.back: Your back is behind you, from your neck to your bottom.
It is the part you lie on to look up.
You feel your back when you lean against a chair.
nouns.body.more.chest: Your chest is the front of your body, above your tummy.
Your heart beats inside your chest.
Your chest goes up and down when you breathe.
nouns.body.more.neck: Your neck holds up your head.
It is between your head and your shoulders.
You turn your neck to look side to side.
nouns.body.more.face: Your face is the front of your head.
It has your eyes, nose, and mouth.
You smile and make faces with your face.
nouns.body.more.cheek: Your cheeks are the soft parts on the sides of your face.
They get rosy and puff out when you smile.
Someone might give your cheek a gentle kiss.
nouns.body.more.chin: Your chin is the bottom of your face.
It is right below your mouth.
Your chin moves up and down when you talk.
nouns.body.more.lip: Your lips are the soft edges of your mouth.
You have a top lip and a bottom lip.
You use your lips to smile and to kiss.
nouns.body.more.tongue: Your tongue is inside your mouth.
It is soft and pink and helps you taste.
You use your tongue to taste and to talk.
nouns.body.face.eyebrow: Your eyebrows are the little lines of hair above your eyes.
You have one over each eye.
Your eyebrows go up when you are surprised.
nouns.body.face.eyelash: Your eyelashes are the tiny hairs on your eyelids.
They are short and curl around your eyes.
Your eyelashes help keep dust out of your eyes.
nouns.body.face.freckle: A freckle is a tiny brown dot on your skin.
Some people have lots, often on the nose and cheeks.
Freckles can show up when you play in the sun.
nouns.body.face.dimple: A dimple is a little dent in your cheek.
It shows up when you give a big smile.
Not everyone has dimples — they are special.
nouns.body.face.beard: A beard is the hair that grows on a grown-up's chin and cheeks.
It can be short and scratchy or long and soft.
Some dads and grandpas have a beard.
nouns.body.face.wrist: Your wrist is where your hand meets your arm.
It bends so your hand can move all around.
You might wear a watch on your wrist.
nouns.body.joints.ankle: Your ankle is where your foot meets your leg.
It bends so your foot can move up and down.
Your ankle helps you walk and balance.
nouns.body.joints.hip: Your hips are where your legs meet your body.
They are on the sides, below your waist.
Your hips help you walk, sit, and wiggle.
nouns.body.joints.waist: Your waist is the middle of your body.
It is where a belt goes around you.
You bend at the waist to touch your toes.
nouns.body.joints.knuckle: A knuckle is a bump where your finger bends.
You have them along each finger.
Your knuckles let your fingers curl into a fist.
